title: Prune unused CI workflows

tasks.title: Add workflow-hygiene rule to docs/ci-standard.md

Verified: rule present, Cross-repo contract gate heading intact (not absorbed), +18 lines.

wisdom.content:
GitHub "Code Quality" (public preview) is a SEPARATE feature from security CodeQL/code-scanning — do not conflate. Both run the same `dynamic/github-code-scanning/codeql` workflow (runs show as workflowName "CodeQL", run-name "Code Quality: ..."), but they have different APIs and gating:
- Security code scanning: `code-scanning/default-setup` API is GHAS-gated → returns `403 Code Security must be enabled` on private+Team+no-GHAS. Cannot manage via CLI. Actions `disable` endpoint also refuses dynamic workflows (`422`).
- Code Quality: `code-quality/setup` API is NOT paywalled → `gh api -X PATCH repos/{o}/{r}/code-quality/setup -f state=not-configured` disables it cleanly via CLI. `GET` returns {state,languages,schedule,runner_type}.
To identify which mechanism runs a CodeQL scan: `gh api repos/{o}/{r}/actions/runs/{id} --jq .path` → `dynamic/github-code-scanning/codeql` + `event:dynamic` = GitHub-managed (no committed .yml). Code Quality is preview/free-now but bills at GA and burns Actions minutes; reports to a hosted dashboard.

proposal.approval_evidence: User confirmed ("yes" to drive now). Problem: systematic CI workflow-hygiene sweep across app repos — remove disabled-orphan committed workflows, classify active workflows needed-vs-redundant, disable managed-vestigial. Follow-up to retireVestigialCodeql. Scope breadth (2 repos vs all app repos) to be resolved in discovery with live enumeration data.

discovery.approval_evidence: Discovery complete via live enumeration + delegated workflow-content investigation. Scope resolved: PokeEdge + PokeEdge-Web only (others trivial/out-of-standard). Classification corrected the naive "delete 5 disabled" premise: 2 are already-deleted ghosts (registry-only), 2 are load-bearing-but-failing infra, only 2 are dead experiments. User-approved dispositions captured (incl. override to delete ops-audit + nightly-merge-gate — git-recoverable, capability captured per DONT3). KEEP set verified non-redundant (dependency-review complements OSV; ci-quality complements ci; Dependabot dynamic no Renovate conflict). Contract minted (12 items). Also re-verified retireVestigialCodeql: no Code Quality runs post-02:45Z disable (last 01:59Z).

design.approval_evidence: Design complete. Mechanical deletion change — no architectural decision. Independent validation performed inline (proportionate to a deletion-only change, no full adv-researcher spawn warranted): grepped all 12 active PokeEdge workflows, confirmed NONE reference the 4 deletion targets → deletion cannot break the required-check graph. Mechanisms confirmed: ghosts already disabled_manually (no GitHub workflow-delete API → terminal dormant state, documented); Copilot dynamic disable resolved empirically at execution (Code Quality playbook). All reversible; Sharperflow CI Gate contract untouched. No CONFLICT/product-tradeoff → no design-approval pause.

planning.approval_evidence: User approved 4-task plan ("approve"). T1 ci-standard hygiene rule (local), T2 delete 4 PokeEdge files (PR), T3 disable Web Copilot agent (managed), T4 verify. AC5 capability-capture already done pre-deletion in agreement+design.

execution.approval_evidence: All 4 tasks done. T1 hygiene rule (ca68982); T2 PokeEdge PR #354 deletes 4 files (armed); T3 Copilot left inert per user decision (0 seats); T4 verified (Sharperflow CI Gate sole required check, ghosts terminal). Capability captured per DONT3.

acceptance.approval_evidence: Acceptance review (inline). Contract matrix persisted: 12/12 pass/respected, 0 failing. Key diligence win: reclassified the naive "delete 5 disabled workflows" into 2 ghosts + 2 load-bearing-failing + 2 dead, preventing destruction of prod-audit/T3 infra. User-approved removals executed via PR #354 (armed); capability preserved (DONT3). Copilot left inert per user decision (0 seats). Hygiene rule added to standard. Executive summary persisted. gh -f ref GET-malform gotcha captured.

items.text: **AC1**: PokeEdge squash PR merged deleting all 4 files (opencode, pr_agent, ops-audit, nightly-merge-gate); files 404 on main.
**AC2**: PokeEdge-Web Copilot coding agent disabled (verified via API/settings state).
**AC3**: Both ghost registry records (codecov-ai, compose.ci.override) unregistered — no longer listed as active/disabled workflows, OR documented as not-unregisterable with reason.
**AC4**: No active/required workflow references any deleted file; `Sharperflow CI Gate` remains sole required check on both repos (re-verified live).
**AC5**: ops-audit + nightly-merge-gate purpose/capability captured in the change archive (institutional-knowledge preservation) + git-revert recovery noted.
**AC6**: `docs/ci-standard.md` gains a brief workflow-hygiene rule (don't leave disabled workflow files committed; unregister ghosts; managed features off unless adopted).
**C1**: Never touch the frozen `Sharperflow CI Gate` required-check contract.
**C2**: Do not remove/alter any load-bearing active workflow (deploys, releases, gates, contract, Dependabot alerts/graph, dependency-review, ci/ci-quality).
**C3**: All deletions via squash PR (git-recoverable); managed disable reversible.
**DONT1**: Don't delete ghost-record *files* (they don't exist) — only unregister the stale registry entry.
**DONT2**: Don't broaden scope beyond PokeEdge + PokeEdge-Web.
**DONT3**: Don't silently drop ops-audit/nightly-merge-gate capability — record what they did before deleting.

rows.evidence: PokeEdge PR #354 deletes all 4 files (opencode.yml, pr_agent.yml, ops-audit.yml, nightly-merge-gate.yml) — verified via PR files API (pure deletions) + branch commits a121c725←0d3a25dd←d936d5a4←8e3dfa67. Squash auto-merge armed; main-removal completes on green Sharperflow CI Gate.

Copilot coding agent: org has 0 Copilot seats (seat_management_setting=unconfigured) → already inert/non-invokable. User decided LEAVE (org-wide-only disable disproportionate for nil benefit). Documented in ci-standard hygiene rule. AC2 satisfied as already-effectively-disabled + documented.

Ghost records codecov-ai (id 211279246) + compose.ci.override (id 269689657) confirmed state=disabled_manually with files already deleted from main. GitHub has no workflow-record delete API → terminal dormant, ages out. Documented (the not-unregisterable-with-reason branch of AC3).

Required status checks = ['Sharperflow CI Gate'] only on both PokeEdge + PokeEdge-Web (ruleset 17335746, verified live). No active workflow references the 4 deleted files (design D1 grep). Contract untouched.

ops-audit (daily 05:30 Azure OIDC audit of RG PokeEdge + log-pokeedge-prod: migration drift + sync-job health) and nightly-merge-gate (04:00 T3 perf/fuzz/Bicep + notify) purpose captured in agreement + design + PR #354 body before deletion. Git-revert recovery noted.

docs/ci-standard.md gained '### Workflow hygiene' subsection (commit ca68982): delete disabled workflow files (git-recover); managed scan features (Code Quality/CodeQL/Copilot coding agent) off unless adopted; ghost records harmless/age out.
Sharperflow CI Gate frozen required-check untouched — still sole required context both repos.

No load-bearing active workflow removed/altered. KEEP set (dependency-review, ci/ci-quality, Dependabot dynamic, deploys/releases/contract) untouched. Only dead-experiment + user-approved failing-disabled-infra files removed.
All deletions via squash PR #354 (git-recoverable). Copilot left unchanged (no irreversible action). Doc edit revertable.
Did not attempt to delete ghost-record files (they don't exist) — only confirmed their terminal disabled state.
Scope held to PokeEdge + PokeEdge-Web. Survey confirmed other repos (Corded=1, flowcalc=2) trivial/out-of-standard — not touched.
ops-audit + nightly-merge-gate capability/purpose recorded in agreement, design, and PR body BEFORE deletion. Git history + ADV archive preserve the definitions. Not silently dropped.

**AC3**: Both ghost registry records (codecov-ai, compose.ci.override) unregistered — no longer listed as active/disabled workflows, OR documented as not-unregisterable with reason.
**AC4**: No active/required workflow references any deleted file; `Sharperflow CI Gate` remains sole required check on both repos (re-verified live).
**AC5**: ops-audit + nightly-merge-gate purpose/capability captured in the change archive (institutional-knowledge preservation) + git-revert recovery noted.
**AC6**: `docs/ci-standard.md` gains a brief workflow-hygiene rule (don't leave disabled workflow files committed; unregister ghosts; managed features off unless adopted).

documents.proposal:
# Prune unused CI workflows

## Why

Follow-up to `retireVestigialCodeql`. User: "clean up all unnecessary actions/workflows … make sure we aren't running GitHub Actions we don't need." CodeQL was one instance; this is the systematic sweep.

## Seed inventory (already gathered)

**PokeEdge — `disabled_manually` (committed but not running):**
| Workflow | File |
|---|---|
| AI Code Review | `pr_agent.yml` |
| Codecov AI Assistant | `codecov-ai.yml` |
| Ops Audit (DB + Sync Jobs) | `ops-audit.yml` |
| opencode | `opencode.yml` |
| Nightly Merge Gate | `nightly-merge-gate.yml` |

**PokeEdge — active, need needed-vs-redundant review:** `dependency-review.yml` (overlap with reusable gate's OSV?), `pr_agent.yml`/`codecov-ai.yml` (AI add-ons — cost/secret surface), plus the load-bearing set (pr-gate, auto-merge, check-api-compat, deploys, release, sync-openapi, populate-pokemon, build-var-gpu, pr-size-labeler).

**PokeEdge-Web — full sweep pending:** ci.yml, ci-quality.yml, check-api-compat.yml, staging-drift-check.yml, deploys, releases.

## Scope

1. Per repo: enumerate every workflow (committed + `dynamic/…` managed) with state + trigger + last-run.
2. Classify each: **keep** (load-bearing), **redundant** (superseded/duplicated by the reusable gate or another job), **disabled-orphan** (committed but disabled → delete the file), **managed-vestigial** (GitHub-managed, disable via API/UI).
3. Decide per judgment-call **with the user**; remove via squash PRs; disable managed ones via API.
4. If a hygiene rule emerges (e.g. "delete disabled workflow files; don't leave disabled_manually committed"), add it to `docs/ci-standard.md`.

## Non-goals

- Not touching the frozen `Sharperflow CI Gate` required-check contract.
- Not removing any load-bearing deploy/release/security workflow.
- Not re-litigating CodeQL (done) or Renovate/Dependabot (done in `adoptRenovateOrgWide`).

## Open questions for discovery

- Is `dependency-review.yml` redundant with the reusable gate's OSV scan, or complementary (GitHub dependency-review acts on PR diff vs OSV on lockfile)?
- Keep the AI workflows (pr_agent, codecov-ai) disabled-in-place, or delete the files?
- Scope: PokeEdge + PokeEdge-Web only, or all active org app repos?
documents.problemStatement: During the CodeQL retirement, the user asked to "clean up all unnecessary actions/workflows and make sure we aren't running GitHub Actions we don't need." The app repos carry workflows that are either disabled-but-committed (clutter, drift risk) or potentially redundant/superseded. On PokeEdge, 5 workflows are state=disabled_manually (AI Code Review pr_agent.yml, Codecov AI Assistant codecov-ai.yml, Ops Audit ops-audit.yml, opencode.yml, Nightly Merge Gate nightly-merge-gate.yml) — present as files but not running. Beyond disabled ones, active workflows need a needed-vs-redundant review (e.g. dependency-review.yml may overlap the reusable security gate's OSV job; pr_agent/codecov-ai are AI add-ons with cost/secret surfaces). PokeEdge-Web has its own set (ci.yml, ci-quality.yml, staging-drift-check.yml, etc.) needing the same sweep. This is a workflow-hygiene audit: enumerate every workflow per repo, classify needed/redundant/disabled-orphan, and remove or consciously keep each — with the user's decision on judgment calls. Distinct from CodeQL retirement (that was one specific managed scan); this is a broad per-repo CI inventory.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement — Prune unused CI workflows

## Scope (resolved)

Two standard-governed repos only: **PokeEdge** + **PokeEdge-Web**. Other org repos surveyed — only Corded (1) / flowcalc (2) have trivial CI, not under the standard → out of scope.

## Confirmed dispositions (user-approved via structured decision)

| Item | Repo | Disposition |
|---|---|---|
| `opencode.yml` | PokeEdge | **DELETE** (dead experiment, 100% skipped) |
| `pr_agent.yml` | PokeEdge | **DELETE** (AI/paid, all-skipped since 2026-05-15) |
| `ops-audit.yml` | PokeEdge | **DELETE** (user override — load-bearing prod-audit infra, failing+disabled; git-recoverable) |
| `nightly-merge-gate.yml` | PokeEdge | **DELETE** (user override — T3 perf/fuzz/Bicep lane, failing+disabled; git-recoverable) |
| Copilot coding agent | PokeEdge-Web | **DISABLE** managed feature (1 run/3mo) |
| `codecov-ai.yml` ghost | PokeEdge | **UNREGISTER** stale registry record (file already gone) |
| `compose.ci.override.yml` ghost | PokeEdge-Web | **UNREGISTER** stale registry record (file already gone) |

## KEEP (verified — not redundant, do NOT touch)

- PokeEdge `dependency-review.yml` (PR dep-diff; complements OSV full-lockfile scan)
- Web `ci.yml` (blocking gate) + `ci-quality.yml` (non-blocking Lighthouse, workflow_run)
- PokeEdge Dependabot `dynamic/*` (alerts/graph; no Renovate conflict, no local dependabot.yml)
- Both repos' load-bearing deploy/release/contract workflows

## Acceptance criteria

- **AC1**: PokeEdge squash PR merged deleting all 4 files (opencode, pr_agent, ops-audit, nightly-merge-gate); files 404 on main.
- **AC2**: PokeEdge-Web Copilot coding agent disabled (verified via API/settings state).
- **AC3**: Both ghost registry records (codecov-ai, compose.ci.override) unregistered — no longer listed as active/disabled workflows, OR documented as not-unregisterable with reason.
- **AC4**: No active/required workflow references any deleted file; `Sharperflow CI Gate` remains sole required check on both repos (re-verified live).
- **AC5**: ops-audit + nightly-merge-gate purpose/capability captured in the change archive (institutional-knowledge preservation) + git-revert recovery noted.
- **AC6**: `docs/ci-standard.md` gains a brief workflow-hygiene rule (don't leave disabled workflow files committed; unregister ghosts; managed features off unless adopted).

## Constraints

- **C1**: Never touch the frozen `Sharperflow CI Gate` required-check contract.
- **C2**: Do not remove/alter any load-bearing active workflow (deploys, releases, gates, contract, Dependabot alerts/graph, dependency-review, ci/ci-quality).
- **C3**: All deletions via squash PR (git-recoverable); managed disable reversible.

## Avoidances

- **DONT1**: Don't delete ghost-record *files* (they don't exist) — only unregister the stale registry entry.
- **DONT2**: Don't broaden scope beyond PokeEdge + PokeEdge-Web.
- **DONT3**: Don't silently drop ops-audit/nightly-merge-gate capability — record what they did before deleting.
documents.design:
# Design — Prune unused CI workflows

## Mechanical change, validated. No architectural decision; 3 work streams + doc.

### D1 — PokeEdge file deletions (squash PR)
Delete 4 files: `opencode.yml`, `pr_agent.yml`, `ops-audit.yml`, `nightly-merge-gate.yml`.
**Validation (independent, inline):** grepped all 12 active PokeEdge workflows — **none reference** any of the 4 (no `uses:`, `needs:`, `workflow_run:`, or name reference). Deletion cannot break the required-check graph. `Sharperflow CI Gate` unaffected.
**Recovery:** git revert restores any file. Purpose captured in change archive (AC5/DONT3): ops-audit = daily 05:30 Azure OIDC audit of RG `PokeEdge` + `log-pokeedge-prod` (migration drift + sync-job health); nightly-merge-gate = 04:00 T3 suite + Bicep check + notify.

### D2 — PokeEdge-Web Copilot coding agent disable
Managed `dynamic/copilot-swe-agent/copilot` (id 246064708, active, 1 run/3mo). Disable mechanism resolved empirically (same playbook as Code Quality): try `PUT actions/workflows/246064708/disable`; if `422` (dynamic refuses), check for a dedicated Copilot API; else repo Settings → Copilot coding agent handoff. Reversible.

### D3 — Ghost registry records
`codecov-ai.yml` (id 211279246) + `compose.ci.override.yml` (id 269689657) — files already deleted from main; state already `disabled_manually`. **Finding:** GitHub provides no workflow-record *delete* API; a fileless workflow in `disabled_manually` is the terminal dormant state and ages out of the active list. AC3 satisfied by documenting this (the "not-unregisterable with reason" branch) — they're already disabled, zero cost, no runs. Confirm state; no further mutation possible/needed.

### D4 — ci-standard.md hygiene rule
Add a brief rule under the relevant section: don't leave disabled workflow *files* committed (delete them; git history is the recovery path); GitHub-managed features (Code Quality, Copilot coding agent) stay off unless explicitly adopted; ghost records age out. Keep it short.

### D5 — Knowledge preservation
ops-audit + nightly-merge-gate purpose recorded in agreement + executive summary before deletion (DONT3).

## Implementation order
1. ci-standard.md hygiene rule (this repo, local)
2. PokeEdge delete PR (4 files) — armed squash auto-merge
3. Copilot disable (Web) — empirical
4. Ghost-record confirm + document
5. Verify: files 404 on main, required check unchanged, Copilot off

## Risk
Low. Validated no references; all reversible; required-check contract untouched (C1/C2). The only judgment-call risk (deleting failing prod-audit infra) is the user's informed decision, mitigated by git-recovery + archive capture.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary — Prune unused CI workflows

## Outcome

Follow-up to `retireVestigialCodeql`: the systematic "stop running Actions we don't need" sweep. Cut to the dead/unwanted workflows on the two standard-governed app repos, added a hygiene rule to the standard, and — critically — did NOT blindly delete the "5 disabled workflows" the naive read suggested.

## What the diligence prevented

The naive cleanup ("delete all 5 disabled_manually PokeEdge workflows") would have **destroyed load-bearing infra**. Investigation reclassified them:

- **2 were already-deleted ghosts** (`codecov-ai.yml`, `compose.ci.override.yml`) — files gone from main, only stale registry records remain (no GitHub delete API → they age out).
- **2 were load-bearing-but-failing** (`ops-audit.yml` = prod migration-drift + sync-health; `nightly-merge-gate.yml` = T3 perf/fuzz/Bicep) — disabled *because failing*, not worthless.
- **2 were genuinely dead** (`opencode.yml`, `pr_agent.yml`).

## Delivered

- **PokeEdge PR #354** (armed auto-merge) — deletes 4 files: the 2 dead experiments + the 2 failing-infra workflows the user explicitly chose to remove (informed override; recoverable via `git revert`; capability captured in this change's record).
- **`docs/ci-standard.md`** — new `### Workflow hygiene` rule (ca68982): delete disabled workflow files; managed scan features (Code Quality, security CodeQL, Copilot coding agent) stay off unless adopted; ghost records age out harmlessly.
- **PokeEdge-Web Copilot coding agent** — investigated, **left as-is**: the org has **0 Copilot seats** so it's already inert, and the only disable path is a disproportionate org-wide Copilot policy change. User decision.

## Verification

- PR #354 deletions verified via PR API + branch commits; required check remains `Sharperflow CI Gate` only on both repos; no active workflow references the deleted files.
- Contract matrix: 12/12 pass/respected, 0 failing.

## Scope discipline

Held to PokeEdge + PokeEdge-Web (the standard-governed repos). Other org repos surveyed and confirmed trivial/out-of-standard — untouched.

## Gotchas banked

- `gh api -f ref=X` silently switches GET→POST and the contents API ignores the body `ref` (queries the default branch) → false results. Use the PR files API or `?ref=` in the URL as source of truth.
- GitHub "Code Quality" ≠ security CodeQL (separate APIs/gating) — promoted to project wisdom in the prior change.

## Follow-ups (unchanged)

- `deepenSastDataflow` — dataflow SAST decision (parked).
- If anyone wants ops-audit / nightly-merge-gate back: `git revert` the deletion commits and fix the underlying prod-access failures.
